summary: Advanced the Old Yoshi stage decomp in `src/melee/gr/groldyoshi.c`, with the PR title claiming 95%. The patch defined stage callback/data tables, added typed Old Yoshi cloud and guest ground-variable layouts, implemented the cloud init/update/collision path and guest behavior functions, and tightened header prototypes. The decomp-dev bot reported new full matches for `grOldYoshi_8020EAFC`, `.data`, `.sdata`, and `grOldYoshi_8020E854`, plus large partial improvements for `grOldYoshi_8020EC10`, `grOldYoshi_8020EFCC`, `grOldYoshi_8020F088`, and `grOldYoshi_8020F31C`.
key_files: src/melee/gr/groldyoshi.c (+299/-30): defined `grOy_803E6488`, `grOy_803E650C`, `grOy_803E6574`, `grOy_803B83F0`, expanded `grOy_804D6A88`, and implemented Old Yoshi cloud/guest functions.
src/melee/gr/types.h (+21/-12): replaced the earlier `grOldYoshi_Entry`/`oldyoshi` layout with `grOldYoshi_Cloud_GroundVars` and `grOldYoshi_Guest_GroundVars` union members.
src/melee/gr/groldyoshi.h (+2/-4): removed unused forward includes and changed `fn_8020F2A8`/`grOldYoshi_8020F31C` from placeholder or typedef-heavy prototypes to concrete `float`-based signatures.
what_changed: Replaced `extern StageCallbacks grOy_803E6488[]` with a concrete six-entry callback table covering the existing `grOldYoshi_*` stage object callbacks.
Added `StageData grOy_803E650C` for stage id 29, callback table `grOy_803E6488`, data file `/GrOy.dat`, stage lifecycle callbacks, dynamics callback, and collision callback.
Expanded `grOy_804D6A88` from a one-int placeholder into a parameter block with `s16` timers/indices and `float` movement values: `x0`, `x2`, `x4`, `x8`, `xC`, `x10`, `x12`, `x14`, `x16`, `x18`.
Defined the joint-pair data as flat `s16 grOy_803E6574[] = {0, 1, 1, 5, 2, 9}` and used it for cloud JObj lookup and map-joint callback registration.

Implemented `grOldYoshi_8020EAFC` to initialize three cloud entries, cache each cloud JObj and initial Y translation, register `fn_8020F2A8` with `mpJointSetCb1`, run ground/anime setup, and set `gp->x11_flags.b012 = 1`.

Implemented `grOldYoshi_8020EC10` as a three-cloud state machine using bitfields at `xC4`, velocity/offset floats `xD4` and `xD0`, animation transitions via `grAnime_801C7FF8`/`grAnime_801C83D0`, effect spawn `grLib_801C97DC`, map-joint removal/addition via `mpLib_80057BC0` and `mpJointListAdd`, and per-frame `HSD_JObjSetTranslateY` updates.

Implemented guest-object setup and update through `grOldYoshi_8020EFCC` and `grOldYoshi_8020F088`, including hiding/showing JObjs with flag `0x10`, random respawn timing via `HSD_Randi`, random Y offset via `HSD_Randf`, and a five-entry guest selection table `grOy_803B83F0`.
Reworked `fn_8020F2A8` so the collision callback checks `coll->x34_flags.b1234 == 1`, maps the joint id through `grOy_803E6574`, and sets the matching cloud's `xC4_4` contact flag.
Implemented `grOldYoshi_8020F31C` as a float helper for accelerating/decelerating toward a target with min/max clamping, replacing the placeholder declaration in the header.

Added concrete Old Yoshi ground-variable structs: `grOldYoshi_Cloud` with packed state/contact/timer fields plus JObj and movement floats, `grOldYoshi_Cloud_GroundVars`, and `grOldYoshi_Guest_GroundVars` with `s16 xC4`/`xC6`.
Updated includes to use real dependencies (`gr/grlib.h`, `mp/mplib.h`, `baselib/random.h`) and removed `lb/forward.h`/`mp/forward.h` from `groldyoshi.h`.
smart_moves: Defining `StageCallbacks` and `StageData` in the C file, rather than keeping them as externs, directly paid off in the bot report: `.data` became 100% matched and `.sdata` became 100% matched.

Splitting Old Yoshi ground state into cloud-specific and guest-specific union views made the same `Ground::gv` area usable for two different callback groups without forcing a single misleading struct.
Modeling the cloud `xC4` storage as bitfields (`xC4_0123`, `xC4_4`, `xC4_567`) preserved the state/contact/timer access pattern needed by both the updater and collision callback.
Using a flat `s16` array for `grOy_803E6574` and indexing `i * 2` matched the visible data layout and simplified both `Ground_801C3FA4` JObj lookup and `mpJointSetCb1` registration.

The cloud update code mirrors the runtime lifecycle carefully: collision sets a one-frame contact flag, the update function consumes it, transitions animation/collision states, updates JObj translation, then resets the flag.

Converting `grOldYoshi_8020F31C` from `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` to an explicit six-`float` signature made the movement helper reusable from the matched cloud state machine and improved type evidence in the header.
decomp_lessons: Stage decomp progress often requires matching data tables and `Ground::gv` layouts at the same time as functions; this PR got function, `.data`, `.sdata`, `.rodata`, and `.sdata2` improvements together.

For stage object callbacks, identify which callback-table entry owns which ground-variable interpretation before naming union members; Old Yoshi uses `oldyoshicloud` for cloud platforms and `oldyoshiguest` for the guest object.
Packed state bytes at `gp+C4` should be modeled with bitfield names that reflect the accessed bit ranges until semantic names are proven.

Map-joint callbacks can be decompiled as small flag setters: verify the `CollData` condition, map the joint id to a stage-local index table, set state in `Ground::gv`, and let the per-frame updater do the heavier behavior.
Exact integer widths matter for timers and post-increment comparisons; the diff uses `s16` fields in the parameter block, `u16 uVar3`, and bitfield timer storage for `xC4_567`.

Do not leave placeholder prototypes in headers once call sites prove the type shape; here `grOldYoshi_8020F31C` became `float(float,float,float,float,float,float)` and the collision callback parameter type became `float`.
naming_conventions: Functions retain the address-based stage prefix `grOldYoshi_8020....`.
Old Yoshi data symbols use the short stage prefix `grOy_`, as in `grOy_803E6488`, `grOy_803E650C`, `grOy_803E6574`, `grOy_804D6A88`, and `grOy_803B83F0`.
Temporary struct fields are offset/bit-range based when semantics are not proven: `xC4_0123`, `xC4_4`, `xC4_567`, `xC8`, `xCC`, `xD0`, `xD4`.
Ground union member names distinguish object roles: `oldyoshicloud` and `oldyoshiguest` rather than the removed generic `oldyoshi`.
The collision callback kept the local helper-style name `fn_8020F2A8`; no human review comments in the slice requested a semantic rename.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: `PAD_STACK(4)` was added in `grOldYoshi_8020EAFC`, indicating a stack-shape match tactic for the newly matched init function.
The cloud updater uses `uVar3 = field++` before threshold comparisons, preserving post-increment ordering for timer state transitions.
The motion helper `grOldYoshi_8020F31C` explicitly computes absolute values with branches instead of library calls, then clamps against the passed positive and negative velocity bounds.
The random timing helper `rand_inline(int a, int b)` wraps `HSD_Randi` in an inline range-selection pattern used by `grOldYoshi_8020EFCC` and `grOldYoshi_8020F088`.
`grOldYoshi_8020F088` copies `grOy_803B83F0[5]` into a local stack array before selecting `local34[HSD_Randi(5)]`, a stack/local-data pattern relevant to its 90.41% partial match.
JObj visibility is controlled with flag `0x10`/`16` through `HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll` and `HSD_JObjClearFlagsAll`; the cloud code and guest code both use this flag pattern.
The collision callback loops over exactly three flat joint-pair entries and exits when the joint is not one of `grOy_803E6574[i*2]`, matching the three-cloud structure.
review_feedback: No human review comments or formal reviews are present in the PR slice.
decomp-dev bot report for GALE01 showed matched code 56.22% (+0.01%, +500 bytes) and matched data 37.53% (+0.02%, +264 bytes).
Bot-reported new full matches: `grOldYoshi_8020EAFC` (+268 bytes), `.data` (+248 bytes), `grOldYoshi_8020E854` (+25 bytes), and `.sdata` (+16 bytes).

Bot-reported partial improvements remaining unmatched: `grOldYoshi_8020EC10` 95.82%, `grOldYoshi_8020F088` 90.41%, `grOldYoshi_8020F31C` 79.74%, `grOldYoshi_8020EFCC` 98.00%, `.rodata` 90.91%, and `.sdata2` 80.00%.
